source: MathPix Markdown documentation audit — syntax reference, GitHub README, tables/figures/lists/theorems docs, library API docs, release notes

features.name: aligned
features.notes: Used for multi-line equations with alignment points inside other math environments. Not directly documented by MMD but supported via MathJax.

features.name: align
features.notes: Numbered multi-line equation alignment. Explicitly documented in MMD syntax reference.

features.name: align*
features.notes: Unnumbered variant of align. Explicitly documented in MMD syntax reference.

features.name: equation
features.notes: Numbered single equation. Explicitly documented in MMD syntax reference.

features.name: equation*
features.notes: Unnumbered single equation. Explicitly documented in MMD syntax reference.

features.name: split
features.notes: Splits a single equation across multiple lines. Can be wrapped in equation environment for numbering. Explicitly documented.

features.name: gather
features.notes: Numbered consecutive equations without alignment. Explicitly documented.

features.name: gather*
features.notes: Unnumbered variant of gather. Explicitly documented.

features.name: cases
features.notes: Piecewise function definitions. Supported via MathJax.

features.name: array
features.notes: General-purpose math array/matrix with alignment specs. Supported via MathJax.

features.name: matrix
features.notes: Plain matrix (no delimiters). Supported via MathJax.

features.name: pmatrix
features.notes: Matrix with parentheses. Supported via MathJax.

features.name: bmatrix
features.notes: Matrix with square brackets. Supported via MathJax.

features.name: Bmatrix
features.notes: Matrix with curly braces. Supported via MathJax.

features.name: vmatrix
features.notes: Matrix with single vertical bars (determinant). Supported via MathJax.

features.name: Vmatrix
features.notes: Matrix with double vertical bars (norm). Supported via MathJax.

features.name: smallmatrix
features.notes: Compact matrix for inline use. Supported via MathJax.

features.name: tabular
features.notes: Primary LaTeX table environment. Extensively documented in MMD. Supports column specs (l, c, r, p{width}), \hline, \multicolumn, \multirow, \cline, \hhline, \diagbox, \backslashbox.

features.name: table
features.notes: Float wrapper for tabular. Supports \caption and \label. Documented in MMD tables page.

features.name: longtable
features.notes: Multi-page table environment. Supports \endfirsthead, \endhead, \endfoot, \endlastfoot.

features.name: center
features.notes: Centre-alignment wrapper. Documented in MMD tables examples.

features.name: figure
features.notes: Float wrapper for images. Supports \caption, \label, \centering, \captionsetup. Explicitly documented in MMD figures page.

features.name: itemize
features.notes: Unordered list environment. Explicitly documented. Supports nested lists to depth of 4.

features.name: enumerate
features.notes: Ordered list environment. Explicitly documented. Supports nested lists and numbering styles.

features.name: abstract
features.notes: Document abstract. Explicitly documented in MMD syntax reference.

features.name: proof
features.notes: Mathematical proof. QED symbol at end. Documented in theorems and proofs page.

features.name: theorem
features.notes: Theorem environment. Requires \newtheorem declaration. Documented in theorems page.

features.name: lemma
features.notes: Lemma environment. Requires \newtheorem declaration. Documented in theorems page.

features.name: definition
features.notes: Definition environment. Requires \newtheorem declaration. Documented in theorems page.

features.name: corollary
features.notes: Corollary environment. Requires \newtheorem declaration.

features.name: remark
features.notes: Remark environment (typically unnumbered via \newtheorem*). Documented in theorems page.

features.name: proposition
features.notes: Proposition environment. Requires \newtheorem declaration.

features.name: lstlisting
features.notes: LaTeX code listing environment. Supports language option and mathescape. Documented in README (added Dec 2025).

features.name: multline
features.notes: Multi-line equation with first line left-aligned, last line right-aligned. Supported via MathJax.

features.name: multline*
features.notes: Unnumbered variant of multline.

features.name: eqnarray
features.notes: Legacy equation array. Generally deprecated in favour of align, but may appear in OCR output.

features.name: eqnarray*
features.notes: Unnumbered variant of eqnarray.

features.name: subequations
features.notes: Groups equations with sub-numbering (1a, 1b, etc.).

features.name: gathered
features.notes: Gathered environment for use inside other math environments. Supported via MathJax.

features.name: flalign
features.notes: Full-width alignment. Supported via MathJax.

features.name: flalign*
features.notes: Unnumbered variant of flalign.

features.name: generic_environment_detector
features.notes: Catch-all regex to detect ANY \begin{} environment not covered above. The analyser should use this as a fallback to flag unknown environments.

math_delimiter.description: Delimiters for inline and block mathematics

features.name: inline_dollar
features.notes: Standard inline maths delimiter. Explicitly documented.

features.name: inline_paren
features.notes: LaTeX inline maths delimiter. Explicitly documented. Document's choice between $...$ and \(...\) should be preserved.

features.name: block_double_dollar
features.notes: Block display maths. Explicitly documented. Non-numbered.

features.name: block_bracket
features.notes: LaTeX block display maths. Explicitly documented. Non-numbered.

heading.description: Section headings and document structure commands

features.name: title_latex
features.notes: LaTeX document title. Always renders centre-aligned. Explicitly documented.

features.name: author
features.notes: Document author. No Markdown equivalent. Explicitly documented.

features.name: section
features.notes: Equivalent to ## in Markdown or <h2>. Explicitly documented.

features.name: subsection
features.notes: Equivalent to ### in Markdown or <h3>. Explicitly documented.

features.name: subsubsection
features.notes: Equivalent to #### in Markdown or <h4>. Explicitly documented.

features.name: markdown_heading
ocrMisreads: # misread as H or number sign dropped
features.notes: Markdown headings H1-H6. # renders left-aligned (unlike \title). Explicitly documented.

features.name: setext_heading_h1
features.notes: Alternative H1 heading via underline. Documented in syntax reference.

features.name: setext_heading_h2
features.notes: Alternative H2 heading via underline. Documented in syntax reference.

table.description: Table syntax — both Markdown and LaTeX formats

features.name: markdown_pipe_table
features.notes: Standard Markdown pipe table. Column alignment via : in separator row. Explicitly documented.

features.name: hline
features.notes: Horizontal line in tabular. Explicitly documented.

features.name: cline
features.notes: Partial horizontal line spanning columns i to j.

features.name: hhline
features.notes: Enhanced horizontal line with fine control. Documented in MMD tables page.

features.name: multicolumn
features.notes: Merge multiple columns. Extensively documented in MMD tables page.

features.name: multirow
features.notes: Merge multiple rows. Documented in MMD tables page.

features.name: diagbox
features.notes: Diagonal split cell header. Supports [dir=SW] option. Documented in release notes.

features.name: centering
features.notes: Centre alignment command used inside figure/table environments.

features.name: caption
features.notes: Table/figure caption. Explicitly documented for both tables and figures.

features.name: captionsetup
features.notes: Caption formatting options. Supports labelformat=empty, singlelinecheck. Documented in figures page.

features.name: markdown_image
features.notes: Standard Markdown image. Explicitly documented.

features.name: markdown_image_with_params
features.notes: MMD extension: additional parameters for width, height, alignment. Supports width=50%, width="36px", right, left, align="left". Explicitly documented.

features.name: includegraphics
features.notes: LaTeX image inclusion. Options: width, height, left/center/right. Default is centre-aligned. Extensively documented in MMD figures page.

features.name: mathpix_cdn_url
features.notes: MathPix CDN image URLs. Common in OCR-processed documents. Preserve exactly.

features.name: mathpix_s3_url
features.notes: MathPix S3 image URLs. Found in documentation examples.

formatting.description: Text formatting — bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, etc.

features.name: bold_markdown_asterisk
features.notes: Markdown bold. Explicitly documented.

features.name: bold_markdown_underscore
features.notes: Alternative Markdown bold. Explicitly documented.

features.name: bold_latex
features.notes: LaTeX bold. Explicitly documented.

features.name: italic_markdown_asterisk
features.notes: Markdown italic. Explicitly documented.

features.name: italic_markdown_underscore
features.notes: Alternative Markdown italic. Explicitly documented.

features.name: italic_latex
features.notes: LaTeX italic. Explicitly documented.

features.name: emph
features.notes: LaTeX emphasis (context-dependent italic/upright).

features.name: strikethrough
features.notes: Markdown strikethrough. Explicitly documented.

features.name: mark
features.notes: Marked/highlighted text. Explicitly documented.

features.name: underline
features.notes: LaTeX underline. Documented in README.

features.name: uline
features.notes: Alternative underline command. Documented in README.

features.name: uuline
features.notes: Double underline. Documented in README.

features.name: uwave
features.notes: Wavy underline. Documented in README.

features.name: dashuline
features.notes: Dashed underline. Documented in README.

features.name: dotuline
features.notes: Dotted underline. Documented in README.

features.name: sout
features.notes: Strikethrough (horizontal line through centre). Documented in README.

features.name: xout
features.notes: Hatching pattern strikethrough. Documented in README.

features.name: inline_code
features.notes: Inline code span. Standard Markdown.

features.name: code_block_fenced
features.notes: Fenced code block with optional language. Syntax highlighting via highlight.js. Explicitly documented.

features.name: subscript_markdown
features.notes: Markdown subscript. Documented in syntax reference.

features.name: superscript_markdown
features.notes: Markdown superscript. Documented in syntax reference.

features.name: label
features.notes: Reference anchor. Used with equations, figures, tables, theorems. Explicitly documented.

features.name: ref
features.notes: Reference to a labelled element. Renders as the number. Explicitly documented.

features.name: eqref
features.notes: Equation reference. Renders as (number). Explicitly documented.

features.name: tag
features.notes: Custom equation tag. Overrides automatic numbering. Explicitly documented.

features.name: url_latex
features.notes: LaTeX URL. Explicitly documented.

features.name: href
features.notes: LaTeX hyperlink with display text.

features.name: markdown_link
features.notes: Standard Markdown link. Documented in syntax reference.

features.name: footnote_inline
features.notes: LaTeX inline footnote. Auto-increments counter. Documented in README.

features.name: footnotemark
features.notes: Footnote marker without text. Documented in README.

features.name: footnotetext
features.notes: Footnote text associated with previous footnotemark. Documented in README.

features.name: markdown_footnote
features.notes: Markdown-style footnote reference and definition.

chemistry.description: Chemistry notation — SMILES strings and chemical equations

features.name: smiles_inline
features.notes: Inline SMILES chemical formula. MMD-specific extension. Explicitly documented.

features.name: smiles_block
features.notes: Block SMILES chemical formula. MMD-specific extension. Explicitly documented.

features.name: ce
features.notes: mhchem chemistry notation (e.g. \ce{H2O}, \ce{CO2 + H2O -> H2CO3}). Supported via MathJax mhchem extension.

list.description: List syntax — ordered and unordered in both Markdown and LaTeX

features.name: markdown_unordered
features.notes: Markdown unordered list. Supports nesting via 2-space indent. Explicitly documented.

features.name: markdown_ordered
features.notes: Markdown ordered list. Numbering continues from first number. Explicitly documented.

features.name: latex_item
features.notes: LaTeX list item marker. Used inside itemize/enumerate. Explicitly documented.

metadata.description: Document metadata, annotations, page breaks, comments

features.name: newtheorem
features.notes: Theorem environment declaration. Supports optional counter argument. Explicitly documented.

features.name: theoremstyle
features.notes: Sets formatting style for subsequent \newtheorem declarations. Styles: plain, definition, remark. Documented.

features.name: pagebreak
features.notes: Page break command. Reflected in LaTeX, PDF, DOCX exports. Explicitly documented in syntax reference.

features.name: latex_comment
features.notes: LaTeX comment. Everything after % on a line is ignored.

features.name: nonumber
features.notes: Suppresses equation numbering for a specific line.

features.name: qed_symbol
features.notes: Custom QED symbol for proofs. Documented in theorems page.

features.name: renewcommand_qedsymbol
features.notes: Redefine QED symbol. Documented in theorems page.

other.description: Miscellaneous features — blockquotes, horizontal rules, hints, emojis, HTML

features.name: blockquote
features.notes: Markdown blockquote. Supports nesting with >>. Documented.

features.name: horizontal_rule
features.notes: Horizontal divider line. Documented.

features.name: hint_collapsible
features.notes: MMD-specific collapsible/hint section. Explicitly documented.

features.name: emoji
features.notes: Emoji shortcodes. Documented in syntax reference.

features.name: html_tags
features.notes: Raw HTML tags. Requires htmlTags=true option. Documented. Supports SVG rendering.

features.name: line_break_backslash
features.notes: Line break in equations (between rows) and general LaTeX line break.

features.name: ampersand_alignment
features.notes: Column separator in tabular and alignment point in align/aligned/array environments.

notationGroups.name: inequality_leq
notationGroups.notes: Document may use any of these — do not normalise between them.

notationGroups.name: inequality_geq
notationGroups.notes: Document may use any of these — do not normalise between them.

notationGroups.name: arrow_right
notationGroups.notes: Different arrows have different semantic meaning. Preserve the original.

notationGroups.name: arrow_left
notationGroups.notes: Preserve original form.

notationGroups.name: arrow_leftright
notationGroups.notes: Preserve original form.

notationGroups.name: arrow_mapsto
notationGroups.notes: Preserve original form.

notationGroups.name: multiplication
notationGroups.notes: Different operators, different meanings. \cdot for scalar, \times for cross product, etc.

notationGroups.name: subset
notationGroups.notes: Strict vs non-strict subset. Preserve original.

notationGroups.name: superset
notationGroups.notes: Strict vs non-strict superset. Preserve original.

notationGroups.name: dots
notationGroups.notes: Baseline vs centred vs vertical vs diagonal dots. Very different meanings.

notationGroups.name: negation_not
notationGroups.notes: Preserve original form.

notationGroups.name: set_membership
notationGroups.notes: Preserve original form.

notationGroups.name: infinity
notationGroups.notes: \infinity is non-standard but sometimes appears in OCR output.

notationGroups.name: sum_product
notationGroups.notes: Very different operators. Preserve original.

notationGroups.name: integral_variants
notationGroups.notes: Single vs double vs triple vs contour integrals.

notationGroups.name: partial_nabla
notationGroups.notes: Different operators. Preserve original.

notationGroups.name: norm_abs
notationGroups.notes: Single bars for absolute value, double bars for norms. Preserve original delimiters.

notationGroups.name: bracket_variants
notationGroups.notes: Delimiter sizing commands. Preserve original sizing choice.

notationGroups.name: approx_sim
notationGroups.notes: Different relations. Preserve original.

notationGroups.name: prec_succ
notationGroups.notes: Preserve original ordering relations.

notationGroups.name: inline_math_delimiters
notationGroups.notes: Document's choice of inline maths delimiter should be preserved consistently.

notationGroups.name: block_math_delimiters
notationGroups.notes: Document's choice of block maths delimiter should be preserved consistently.

greekLetters.description: Greek letter commands — all standard LaTeX Greek letters with known OCR misreads

ocrMisreads: δ misread as d or ð
ε misread as e or E
ξ misread as ε or ζ
π misread as n or TT or II
τ misread as t or T
υ misread as v or u
\psi misread as ψ misread as w
Γ misread as F or I
Δ misread as A or triangle
Ξ misread as E or three horizontal lines
Ω misread as O or ohm symbol

greekLetters.notes: Greek letter variant pairs (e.g. \epsilon vs \varepsilon, \theta vs \vartheta, \phi vs \varphi) must be preserved — the document's choice is intentional.

commonOcrSymbolMisreads.description: Common OCR misreads for mathematical symbols beyond Greek letters

misreads: ∑ misread as E or 2 or Σ
∫ misread as f or ∫ glyph corruption
√ misread as V or \/ or surd corruption
∅ misread as 0 or O or ø

libraryCapabilities.description: mathpix-markdown-it is built on markdown-it and exposes several useful methods for parsing and analysis

renderMethods.name: MathpixMarkdownModel.markdownToHTML(str, options)
renderMethods.description: Renders MMD to HTML string. Primary render method.

renderMethods.name: MathpixMarkdownModel.render(str, options)
renderMethods.description: Renders MMD to HTML wrapped in a container. For full documents.

renderMethods.name: MathpixMarkdownModel.parseMarkdownByHTML(html, include_sub_math?)
renderMethods.description: Parses rendered HTML and returns array of format objects ({type, value}). Types include: mathml, asciimath, latex, svg, html, tsv. Can extract nested maths from tables.
renderMethods.notes: Could be used to verify that detected LaTeX environments actually render. However, requires HTML rendering first, so adds overhead.

renderMethods.name: MathpixMarkdownModel.parseMarkdownByElement(htmlElement)
renderMethods.description: Same as parseMarkdownByHTML but takes a DOM element.

libraryCapabilities.notes: The library builds on markdown-it's token system. The underlying markdown-it parser generates tokens in a flat array (not a tree), which could theoretically be accessed for feature detection. However, the library does NOT expose a public tokenisation-only API — you must go through markdownToHTML() or render(). For the analyser module, regex-based detection on raw MMD text will be more practical than attempting to hook into the parser internals. The outMath options (include_latex, include_asciimath, etc.) and parseMarkdownByHTML could potentially be used to validate detected maths, but add significant overhead. The disableRules option reveals some internal rule names: 'replacements', and the renderOptions toggle (enable_markdown, enable_latex, enable_markdown_mmd_extensions) shows the three major parsing categories. The enable_markdown_mmd_extensions controls: smiles, asciiMath, mathML.

cdnUsage.notes: CDN bundle exposes window.loadMathJax() and window.MathpixMarkdownModel. The bundle is substantial (~several MB) so loading it solely for analysis would be excessive.
